title: Conlon–Fox–Rödl Question on hedgehog Ramsey numbers
context_text: Conlon, Fox, and Rödl proved $r(H_t; 2) \leq 4t^3$ and asked whether the two-color Ramsey number of the hedgehog $H_t$ is nearly linear in the number of its vertices $t + \binom{t}{2}$. This is the central open question motivating the paper, set against the backdrop of whether a Burr–Erdős-type bound holds for 1-degenerate 3-uniform hypergraphs under 2-colorings.
paper_contribution: Proves $r(H_t) < 200t^2 \ln t + 400t^2 + 1$, establishing $r(H_t) = O(t^2 \ln t)$ and thus confirming $r(H_t; 2) = t^{2+o(1)}$ affirmatively.

title: 3-uniform 2-color Burr–Erdős analogue
statement_text: Does there exist a constant $c(d)$ depending only on $d$ such that, for every $d$-degenerate 3-uniform hypergraph $H$ on $n$ vertices, $r(H; 2) \leq c(d) \cdot n$?
context_text: The Burr–Erdős conjecture for graphs was proved by Lee. Conlon, Fox, and Rödl showed the 4-uniform analogue is false (the 4-uniform hedgehog $H_t^{(4)}$ is 1-degenerate yet has $r(H_t^{(4)};2) \geq 2^{ct}$) and the 3-uniform analogue fails for 3 or more colors. The paper states that the 3-uniform 2-color case 'remains open' and that it was not previously known whether $r(H_t;2)$ is even near-linear.
paper_contribution: Proves $r(H_t; 2) = O(t^2 \ln t)$ for the hedgehog $H_t$, a canonical 1-degenerate 3-uniform hypergraph, providing the first nearly-linear upper bound for a non-trivial family in this setting.
notes: Stated only in prose as 'remains open' with no formal conjecture environment; the 3-uniform 2-color variant is a natural extension of the original Burr–Erdős conjecture but no specific attribution for this precise variant is given in the paper.
